name: JavaScriptCore DFG
summary: JavaScriptCore's mid-tier JIT IR: SSA with explicit dataflow edges, aggressive speculative type guards, and OSR exits carrying register/stack state to resume in the lower tier. Sits between Baseline and FTL/B3 in JSC's four-tier ladder.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: DFG (Data Flow Graph) is SSA with explicit dataflow edges and aggressive speculative type guards. Each guard records an *OSR exit* (On-Stack Replacement: the mechanism that transitions execution between tiers without unwinding) carrying enough register and stack state to resume in the lower tier.

sections.title: JSC's four-tier ladder
sections.content:
JSC's four tiers are: LLInt (interpreter), Baseline (template JIT), DFG (mid-tier), FTL (Faster Than Light, the top tier built on B3, JSC's lower-level SSA optimizer). DFG is the mid-tier slot; it speculates aggressively based on Baseline-collected feedback but compiles much faster than FTL.

Tier orchestration (when each tier is invoked, OSR transitions, when to recompile) belongs in `data/tiered-compilation`; this record covers the data model only.

sections.title: Explicit dataflow edges
sections.content:
DFG is distinguished from sea-of-nodes IRs (TurboFan, HotSpot C2) by making dataflow edges explicit rather than implicit in the graph structure. Each value has named uses; each use refers to a specific definition. This makes range analyses, constant propagation, and type-narrowing passes more direct, at the cost of more bookkeeping during construction.

For a JIT-tier role, the explicitness is a feature: passes can run in fixed order with predictable cost, which matters when the goal is to compile and ship to the CPU before the user notices.
